params.name: Jilin-1 Meteorite
params.description:
On May 7, 1976 AD, a meteor shower occurred in the Jilin region.
Jilin-1 is the largest stone meteorite in the world, weighing up to 1,770 kilograms. It is estimated that its parent body was a planet from the asteroid belt that formed about 4.6 billion years ago. About 8 million years ago, a celestial body collided with the parent body of the Jilin-1 meteorite, causing it to become a meteor shower and eventually fall to Earth.
